summary: Import-time remote code execution via AES-decrypted URL and Function() eval
details: check-error-util@2.1.6 is a backdoored typosquat of the legitimate chaijs check-error package. On require('check-error-util'), index.js invokes resolveConfig() at module top level. The function XOR-decodes a 160-element constant array with key 87 (disguised behind a fake 'getHashAddress' helper documented as an S-box hex builder) to recover an AES-256-CBC key/IV/ciphertext blob, decrypts it to an HTTPS URL, fetches that URL, JSON.parses the response, and executes the `cookie` field via `new Function('require', <remote>)(require)` — passing the consumer's `require` into attacker-controlled JavaScript. This grants the remote operator full Node module access (filesystem, network, child_process, env) in any process that imports the package. The package replicates chaijs's check-error API surface and reuses chaijs author and git URL metadata to lure developers searching for the legitimate utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
